title: Senior AI Research Engineer
company_focus:
Legal AI technology platform combining legal operating
system with legal intelligence for law firms
description:
Clio is the global leader in legal AI technology, empowering
legal professionals and law firms of every size to work
smarter, faster, and more securely.

We are transforming the legal experience for all by
bettering the lives of legal professionals while increasing
access to justice.

Summary:

Clio signed a definitive agreement to acquire vLex in 2025.
This transaction is a major milestone in Clio’s platform
evolution and a turning point for the future of legal work.
It combines Clio’s legal operating system - trusted by more
than 200,000 legal professionals - with vLex’s cutting-edge
legal intelligence platform, which includes Vincent AI,
built on the industry’s most comprehensive global legal
database. Together, these platforms establish a new category
of intelligent legal technology at the intersection of the
business and practice of law, empowering legal professionals
to seamlessly manage, research, and execute legal work
within a unified system. Learn more here.

This role is available to candidates local to our London
hub, with the expectation of being in office minimum twice
per week on our Anchor Days.

As a Senior AI Research Engineer, you will play a key role
in developing and advancing Vincent, vLex’s groundbreaking
AI, by contributing to applied research experiments that
integrate cutting-edge AI - particularly large language
models (LLMs) - into real-world legal workflows. You will
work closely with software engineers and domain experts on
sophisticated technical challenges to deliver innovative
solutions that push the boundaries of legal technology.

What you’ll work on:

- Foundations of retrieval at scale: Re-think how we search
  over vast, heterogeneous legal corpora. Exploring new
  approaches to chunking, query augmentation, hybrid
  sparse/dense embeddings, contextual embeddings,
  quantisation, to push the boundaries of recall, speed, and
  efficiency
- Learning to rank with legal signals: Experiment with re-
  ranking strategies for search results that incorporate
  legal-specific signals that go beyond keyword matches and
  semantic similarities, to surface the most authoritative
  and relevant results first
- Agentic infrastructure for legal workflows: Develop the
  foundations of agents that can decompose complex legal
  tasks, plan across multiple tools, and execute with
  traceability and guardrails. These are building blocks
  that underpin future end-to-end automated workflows
- Evaluation frameworks for retrieval and agents: Design and
  run experiments that help us measure system performance
  against the needs of legal professionals: offline
  testbeds, human-in-the-loop evaluations, implicit feedback
  pipelines, and alignment between lab metrics and customer
  satisfaction
- Grounding and validation of model responses: Create
  mechanisms that ensure model responses are trustworthy by
  anchoring them in trusted legal sources, with clear
  traceability to supporting evidence, and methods for
  validating cited content and surfacing uncertainty when
  the model lacks sufficient grounding

A day in the life might look like:

- Leading complex research projects focused on LLMs, advanced
  information retrieval, and novel AI applications in the
  legal domain, working independently with minimal
  supervision
- Designing and implementing sophisticated prototypes that
  demonstrate the feasibility and business value of cutting-
  edge research ideas, ensuring they meet production-quality
  standards
- Driving the transition of research innovations from
  prototype to production, collaborating closely with
  product and engineering teams to ensure successful
  integration and scalability
- Contributing to strategic research planning by identifying
  high-impact research opportunities and helping prioritize
  initiatives based on business value and technical
  feasibility
- Staying at the forefront of AI research, particularly in
  NLP, LLMs, and legal AI, actively incorporating state-of-
  the-art techniques into your work and sharing insights
  with the team
- Lead technical discussions and code reviews, ensuring high
  standards of research quality and implementation across
  the team
- Collaborating with cross-functional stakeholders including
  product managers, legal engineers, and business leaders to
  align research efforts with strategic objectives
- Documenting and communicating research findings through
  internal presentations, technical documentation, and
  potentially external publications or conference
  presentations
- Contributing to the development of research infrastructure
  and best practices that enhance the team's overall
  efficiency and impact

What you may have

- Masters degree or PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning,
  Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field
- Industry experience in applied AI research or engineering
- At least one of the following experiences:
  - Information retrieval systems
  - LLM fine-tuning and evaluation
  - LLM hosting and inference optimization
  - LLM workflow automation using prompt engineering, prompt
    chaining techniques, and agent orchestration frameworks
- Track record of independently delivering complex technical
  projects from ideation to implementation
- Hands-on experience in working closely with product
  engineering in production applications
- Proficiency in data manipulation, cleaning, and pre-
  processing for complex unstructured data sets
- A proven ability to quickly learn new technologies and adapt
  to a fast-paced environment with distributed teams and
  customers
- A strong understanding of modern AI tools and approaches
- Clear communication skills and the ability to build trust
  with both internal teams and external customers
- A desire to continuously learn, challenge yourself, and
  refine your craft
- A deep interest in how technology transforms the legal
  industry is highly valuable

This is a new role.

What you will find here:

Compensation is one of the main components of Clio’s Total
Rewards Program. We have developed a series of programs and
processes to ensure we are creating fair and competitive pay
practices that form the foundation of our human and high-
performing culture.


Some highlights of our Total Rewards program include:

- Competitive, equitable salary with top-tier health benefits
  and dental coverage.
- Hybrid work environment, with expectation for local Clions
  (Vancouver, Calgary, Toronto, Dublin and Sydney) to be in
  office min. twice per week.
- Flexible time off policy
- Pension contribution
- Clioversary recognition program with special acknowledgement
  at 3, 5, 7, and 10 years

Please note salary bands may differ based on location and
local currency. Additionally, benefits may differ depending
on the employee's location.

Diversity, Inclusion, Belonging and Equity (DIBE) &
Accessibility

Our team shows up as their authentic selves, and are united
by our mission. We are dedicated to diversity, equity and
inclusion. We pride ourselves in building and fostering an
environment where our teams feel included, valued, and
enabled to do the best work of their careers, wherever they
choose to log in from. We believe that different
perspectives, skills, backgrounds, and experiences result in
higher-performing teams and better innovation. We are
committed to equal employment and we encourage candidates
from all backgrounds to apply.

Clio provides accessibility accommodations during the
recruitment process. Should you require any accommodation,
please let us know and we will work with you to meet your
needs.

Learn more about our culture at clio.com/careers

We're a Human and High Performing AI company, meaning we use
artificial intelligence to improve all of our operations. In
recruitment, AI helps us streamline the process for greater
efficiency. However, we've built our systems to ensure that
a human always reviews AI-generated output, and we never
make automated hiring decisions.

Disclaimer: We only communicate with candidates through
official @clio.com email addresses.
responsibilities: Lead research projects on LLMs and advanced information retrieval for legal applications
Design and implement production-quality prototypes demonstrating research innovations
Drive transition of research innovations from prototype to production deployment
Develop retrieval systems with hybrid sparse/dense embeddings and query augmentation
Build agentic infrastructure for legal workflow automation with guardrails
Design evaluation frameworks for retrieval systems and agents with human-in-the-loop processes
Create mechanisms for grounding and validating LLM responses in legal sources
Contribute to strategic research planning and cross-functional collaboration
use_cases: Legal document search and retrieval across vast legal corpora
Legal research with authoritative result ranking
Automated legal task execution through AI agents
Legal workflow automation with traceability and guardrails
Trustworthy AI responses grounded in legal sources for legal professionals
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves directly working with LLMs for retrieval
systems, fine-tuning, evaluation, and building agentic
infrastructure. The position focuses on advancing Vincent AI
through applied research on LLMs, information retrieval, and
agent orchestration - all core AI/ML work.
